steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "nearly 150 years ago" is an explicit past-time adverb. It places Van Gogh's act of completing the drawing in a finished, specific point in the past.

steps.title: Apply the simple-past rule for past time adverbs — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
"Nearly 150 years ago" requires simple past tense; no other tense is compatible with this adverb.
  • Choice A — "has been completing": present perfect progressive. Present perfect cannot be used with a specific past-time adverb like "years ago." Also implies the action is still ongoing. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "is completing": present progressive. Describes an action happening now, which contradicts "150 years ago." Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "will complete": simple future. Cannot refer to something already done in the past. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "completed": simple past. The only tense that correctly places a finished action at a specific past time. Correct.
